channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Farnaz Fassihi

item.title: This is what happened on March 15.
Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: Five missiles struck the Baghdad International Airport and injured four people. Israeli and Iranian attacks continued as global economic concerns about the war mounted.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A family near a damaged building from U.S.-Israeli strikes in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: 4 Palestinians Killed When Israeli Forces Open Fire on West Bank Family
item.description: Six members of a Palestinian family went out for a ride in the car. Only two made it back home.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Natan Odenheimer and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.media:credit: Majdi Mohammed/Associated Press
item.media:description: Palestinians mourning the deaths of four members of the Odeh family in Tammun, West Bank, on Sunday.

item.title: Entering War’s Third Week, Trump Faces Stark Choices
item.description: As the conflict with Iran expands and intensifies, President Trump’s options — to fight on, or to move toward declaring victory and pulling back — both carry deeply problematic consequences.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger, Eric Schmitt, Tyler Pager, Ronen Bergman and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump has wrestled publicly with his stay-or-leave options, sometimes suggesting that the war is all but won and at others seeming to acknowledge that there is still heavy fighting ahead.

item.title: After Trump’s Call to Send Warships to Strait of Hormuz, Nations Respond With Caution
item.description: President Trump has urged China, Britain, France, Japan and South Korea to send warships to help reopen the waterway, even though they were not involved in the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ravi Mattu
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in the White House on Thursday.

item.title: This is what happened on March 14.
Oil Prices May Not Fall Soon, U.S. Energy Secretary Says
item.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the Strait of Hormuz, a critical passageway for oil shipments, remained unsafe for tankers. Iran has been firing projectiles and laying mines.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: In an interview with ABC News on Sunday, Energy Secretary Chris Wright expressed some confidence that the United States would be able to secure the Strait of Hormuz within weeks.

item.title: Israel Says Michigan Synagogue Attacker’s Brother Was a Hezbollah Commander
item.description: The Israeli military said it killed the brother of Ayman Mohamad Ghazali in an airstrike in Lebanon a week before the attack on the synagogue.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
item.media:description: A sheriff’s vehicle is parked in the driveway of Temple Israel, where an attacker drove his car into the synagogue on Thursday before being killed by armed guards, in West Bloomfield, Mich.

item.title: War in Iran Has Grounded Gulf Airlines Like Emirates
item.description: Emirates, Qatar Airways and Etihad Airways have become some of the world’s largest and most profitable thanks to their location at the center of busy travel routes.
item.dc:creator: Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
item.media:credit: Altaf Qadri/Associated Press
item.media:description: Dubai International Airport early this month. Emirates has become one of the world’s largest and most profitable airlines over the last 40 years.

item.title: A Timeline of the Fraught Relationship Between Iran and the U.S.
item.description: The governments of both countries have repeatedly cast the other as evil, perpetuating a cycle that has culminated in the present war.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Iranians hammering nails in a mock coffin draped with an American flag on Friday at the annual Jerusalem Day rally in Tehran.

item.title: As Rockets Fly Overhead, Residents of Israel’s Border City Stay Underground
item.description: The Israeli government evacuated Kiryat Shmona during the last round of fighting with Hezbollah in 2023. Residents who were told it was safe to return are again under fire.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Amit Elkayam
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Amid War in Iran, Europe’s Flashes of Military Strength Also Show Its Weakness
item.description: To defend allies from Iran, the continent’s powers have mounted a rare show of force. But those efforts have diverted limited resources from other hot spots.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes and Catherine Porter
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France singing the French national anthem in Cyprus on Monday with the crew of an aircraft carrier deployed in the Mediterranean Sea.

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong, Farnaz Fassihi and Ephrat Livni

item.title: Two Arrested in Death of Anti-Iranian Regime Activist in Canada
item.description: A man and a woman were charged with murder in what the authorities described as a “targeted incident” against Masood Masjoody, who had gone missing weeks ago in British Columbia.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang
item.category: Iran Front for the Revival of Law and National Sovereignty
item.media:credit: Sebastien St-Jean/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.
item.dc:creator: Traci Carl and Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: During a rally in Tehran on Friday, a woman held a photo of Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran’s new supreme leader, in front of a banner showing his father, who was killed in recent U.S.-Israeli strikes.

item.title: Three More Iranian Soccer Players Withdraw Bid for Asylum in Australia
item.description: Seven members of the national women’s team had sought refuge in the country after they were labeled “traitors” at home. Four of them have since changed their minds.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Kim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Izhar Khan/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Members of the Iranian women’s national soccer team before a match this month in Australia.

item.title: Warning to Americans to leave Iraq is a sign the Iran war is spilling over the border.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Eve Sampson
item.media:credit: Hadi Mizban/Associated Press
item.media:description: U.S. embassy personnel inspect the damage caused by an attack on the building in Baghdad on Saturday.

item.title: FCC Chair Threatens to Revoke Broadcasters’ Licenses Over Iran War Coverage
item.description: The comment from Brendan Carr came on the heels of a social media message from President Trump criticizing the news media’s coverage of the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Brendan Carr in Washington in January.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the U.S.-Israel War With Iran on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Baghdad was attacked overnight. Israeli strikes continued to hit the capitals of Iran and Lebanon, as Iran refused to relinquish control over a vital oil route.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Officials said that more than 800 people have been killed in Lebanon since the start of the war, with more than 2,100 people killed across the region.

item.title: Tehran Seems Largely Empty, as Many Flee and Others Weary of the Fighting
item.description: Residents say a heavy, lifeless atmosphere hangs over the streets of Iran’s capital, which has endured relentless attacks throughout the war.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Tajrish Bazaar in Tehran on Saturday. More than 1,348 civilians have been killed in Iran since the beginning of the war, according to the country’s representative to the United Nations.

item.title: How a Marine Unit in the Middle East Could Open New Phase of Iran War
item.description: The unit’s arrival in the coming days will give the Pentagon the ability to quickly launch raids.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Gibbons-Neff and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: A Marine Expeditionary Unit during training in Okinawa, Japan, last year.

item.title: Sandstorm Batters Gaza, Slamming Makeshift Shelters
item.description: Millions of Gazans have remained without proper housing since the war, according to the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Dawoud Abu Alkas/Reuters
item.media:description: Displaced Palestinians faced a sandstorm in Gaza City on Saturday.

item.title: The war has put Middle East water supplies at risk.
item.dc:creator: Mira Rojanasakul, Claire Brown and Hiroko Tabuchi

item.title: Dozens of Medical Workers Killed as Israel Hits Lebanon
item.description: Lebanese health officials say that 31 health workers have been killed by Israeli strikes. Israel claimed, without evidence, that Hezbollah has been using ambulances and medical facilities.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Mohammed Zaatari/Associated Press
item.media:description: The funeral for Youssef Assaf, a volunteer with the Lebanese Red Cross, in Tyre, Lebanon, on Wednesday.

item.title: Lebanon’s Displaced Flee Israeli Strikes One Year Into War
item.description: Israeli calls for the evacuation of huge swaths of southern Lebanon have led 830,000 people, about 14 percent of Lebanon’s population, to flee.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A man lighting a fire to keep warm along Beirut’s waterfront, after fleeing the city’s southern suburbs last week.

item.title: This is what happened on March 13.
Netanyahu Has the War He Always Wanted, but on Trump’s Terms
item.description: Israel’s prime minister wanted regime change in Iran. But President Trump seems prepared to settle for something less.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in January at the funeral of an Israeli hostage.

item.title: Tim Kaine Is Forcing the Iran War Debate From the Minority
item.description: Senator Tim Kaine, Democrat of Virginia, has found a way to repeatedly occupy floor time and force all senators to take votes challenging President Trump’s use of military force.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times

item.title: Why Little Was Done to Head Off Oil’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
item.description: Geography and regional rivalries have prevented Gulf countries from finding a true alternative to the strait, which the war with Iran has effectively shut down.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Sahar Al Attar/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A naval vessel sailing this month in the Strait of Hormuz. The waterway remains the only option to export most of the energy produced in the region.

item.title: How Migrant Workers Have Been Affected by Iran’s Strikes
item.description: Since the U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran began, Iran has fired hundreds of missiles and drones in retaliation at Gulf countries. Iranian officials say that they are attacking U.S. military bases and American interests in the Gulf countries, not civilian targets. Our reporter Vivian Nereim talks with Katrin Bennhold about how migrant workers have been affected.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim, Katrin Bennhold, Leila Medina, James Surdam and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump and Rubio’s Vision of War: The Art of Destroy and Deal
item.description: Unleashing his inner hawk, Secretary of State Marco Rubio plans President Trump’s military interventions. So far, they favor regime compliance, not change.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong and Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is at the helm of President Trump’s aggressive campaigns to reshape the governments of Iran, Venezuela, Cuba and beyond.

item.title: What to Know About Kharg Island, Iran’s Key Oil Hub
item.description: The Persian Gulf island, targeted by U.S. strikes, is home to Iran’s main oil export terminal. Any disruption could jolt global energy markets.
item.dc:creator: John Yoon and Megan Specia
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu Agency, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Smoke billowing from a flare stack at the Port of Kharg Island oil terminal in Iran in 2017.

item.title: Here is the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Yeganeh Torbati, Farnaz Fassihi and Erika Solomon

item.title: Trump’s War Alliance With Israel Is Reshaping the Middle East. But It Carries Risks.
item.description: President Trump is the first American leader to embrace fighting a full-fledged, joint war with Israel. Washington has tried to avoid that level of coordination in the past.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at Mar-a-Lago in December.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Friday would be the most intense day of the U.S. air campaign in Iran as a vital oil supply route remained choked off.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A resident walks through a neighborhood of central Beirut, the capital of Lebanon, on Friday.

item.title: U.S. Attacks Iran’s Kharg Island, a Key Port for Oil Exporting, Trump Says
item.description: The strikes on Kharg Island targeted Iranian military forces, not economic infrastructure on the island, a U.S. military official said.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Farnaz Fassihi and Liam Stack
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A satellite image of Kharg Island in Iran last month.

item.title: U.S. Vows to Block Iran’s Attempt to Shut Down Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said that the disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz were “something we are dealing with.” And about 2,500 Marines were headed to the Middle East to bolster the war effort.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Luke Broadwater and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Thousands marched amid airstrikes on Quds Day, a government-sponsored event, on Friday in Tehran.

item.title: A New Trump Envoy Stirs Fears of U.S. Meddling In Brazil’s Elections
item.description: Darren Beattie, the U.S. policy adviser on Brazil, planned to visit former President Jair Bolsonaro in prison and meet his son, who is also seeking the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.media:credit: Rebecca Noble for The New York Times
item.media:description: Darren Beattie, who recently became the State Department’s new senior adviser on Brazil policy, was fired from a job in the first Trump administration for appearing at a conference attended by white nationalists.

item.title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: Lizzie Dearden
item.media:credit: Altaf Qadri/Associated Press
item.media:description: A building in Dubai damaged by debris from an intercepted missile on Friday. The United Arab Emirates prosecutor said last week that taking or sharing videos documenting airstrikes or damage could be illegal.

item.title: More Marines and Warships Being Sent to Middle East, U.S. Officials Say
item.description: Iran’s response to days of aerial bombardment and long-range artillery strikes has proved more resilient than Trump administration officials anticipated.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Navy ships in Nagasaki, Japan, in 2023.

item.title: U.S.-Made Launcher Fired Missiles From Bahrain Toward Iran, Video Shows
item.description: It is unclear from the video alone whether the U.S. or Bahraini military launched the missiles. Iran has frequently accused Persian Gulf countries of allowing their territory to be used as a launchpad for U.S. attacks.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim, Aric Toler and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: To Fight Iran’s Drones, U.S. Taps Ukraine’s Hard-Earned Knowledge
item.description: As Iran has shown, warfare involves far more deadly projectiles than it once did. It’s a problem Ukraine has been dealing with for years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Schwirtz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Kacper Pempel/Reuters
item.media:description: An American soldier, in Poland, carrying an interceptor drone used in the Merops anti-drone system. The system was deployed in Poland after Russian drones crossed into the country.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz is narrow — and shallow enough to lay minefields.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Granados, John Ismay and Agnes Chang

item.title: Read Some of John F. Burns’s Reporting From Around the World
item.description: In a 40-year career as an international correspondent for The New York Times, Mr. Burns had a talent for capturing the sweep of history in intricate detail.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.media:credit: Ozier Muhammad/The New York Times
item.media:description: John F. Burns in 2002 in Afghanistan. Following the attacks of Sept. 11, he coordinated coverage and reported on the war from Kabul and Islamabad, in Pakistan.

item.title: Trump Softens Call for Protesters to Take Over Iran
item.description: President Trump said protesters risk getting shot “right through the head,” a change in tone from his earlier comments that Iranians must seize the chance to take over their government.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Donald J. Trump now says Iranians could face threats for protesting against the government, which his war has failed to topple so far.
